assertions.reason: When initial data requests were held in a browser-isolated failure scenario, the app showed a visible loading overlay instead of a blank or broken shell, and a normal run rendered loaded content afterward.
evidence: Screenshot root-loading-overlay.png captured the root view with the loading message "Loading intelligence data…" while data requests were blocked.
Screenshot loaded-state.png shows the same surface after successful loading with the full map shell, stats, timeline, and interactive controls visible.

assertions.reason: When backend API requests for /admin were aborted inside the browser session, the frontend displayed a user-friendly error state with a Retry action instead of a blank screen, and navigation remained usable.
evidence: Screenshot admin-error-state.png shows the message "Failed to load dashboard data" with a Retry button while the app shell still rendered.
After the error rendered, clicking the Map navigation link successfully returned to the main application route, demonstrating the UI remained interactive.

interactiveChecks.action: Blocked root data requests inside the browser session and opened /.
interactiveChecks.observed: The app displayed a loading overlay with the text "Loading intelligence data…" instead of an empty screen.
The page showed a friendly dashboard error with a Retry button rather than crashing or rendering blank content.

interactiveChecks.action: Clicked the Map navigation link from the admin error state.
interactiveChecks.observed: The app navigated back to the root route, confirming the shell remained interactive after the error.

frictions: The main / route falls back to client-side GDELT fetching, so the admin route provided the fastest deterministic backend-error validation path.
